name: Cheesy Bacon-Corn Muffins
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry the bacon and onion in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat for 5 minutes, or until the bacon is crisp and the onions are tender. Remove the bacon and onion and drain on paper towels.
2. Sift the flour, corn meal, sugar, baking powder, and salt into a bowl and set aside.
3. In another bowl, combine the eggs, milk, and melted butter, blending well.
4. Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture all at once, stirring just until moistened.
5. Stir in the bacon-onion mixture and cheese, blending well.
6. Spoon the batter into greased 3-inch muffin pan cups, filling each 2/3 full.
7. Bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 15 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center of a muffin comes out clean. Cool until cold to the touch and wrap in foil, or serve immediately while still hot.
To Reheat:
1. Heat the foil-wrapped muffins in a preheated 350°F oven for 10 to 12 minutes.
